mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS on 20-day forward returns is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.5 percent, weak labor market conditions suppress consumer spending expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression predicting 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, rising industrial production indices reflect improving corporate output and capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns during identified post-shock recovery periods, with a hit rate below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates strengthening economic activity and wage growth, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth exceeds 2.0 percent, robust macroeconomic expansion boosts equity valuations, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 surpasses 13100.

mechanism_narrative: The joint effect of low inflation and near-zero interest rates creates a favorable real yield environment, where the interaction of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is statistically insignificant or negative in predicting 20-day forward returns in a multivariate model.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing downturn regime, depressed housing starts drag on construction and related sectors, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST values below 600 fail to predict negative 5-day forward returns, with a positive return hit rate exceeding 50% during housing downturn periods.
